summary: Cathy O'Neil shares her plans to attend and volunteer at the Clearwater Festival, a folk music gathering in Croton-on-Hudson. The post is a personal, lighthearted account of her preparations for camping and kitchen volunteer duties. She notes that this year's festival will be a tribute to Pete Seeger, who founded it and passed away earlier that year. She mentions musicians she's excited to see and lists her eclectic packing items including instruments and 'bluegrass juice.'
key_points: The Clearwater Festival was Pete Seeger's creation and this year serves as a tribute following his death
O'Neil is volunteering in the kitchen alongside 1,000 other volunteers
She's bringing her fiddle and banjo, suggesting active participation in the folk music tradition
The post reveals her love of bluegrass and folk music culture outside her data science work
money_quotes: If you've never done that, let me tell you that there's something gross and creepy about wet pillows.
I love doing dishes for some reason.

Stuff I've packed for the trip: tent, sleeping bag, pillow (dry so far), bluegrass juice (of the Jack Daniels variety), my fiddle, my banjo, a wooden bowl and utensils, and some metal coffee cups and shot glasses.
You should totally come by for either day or for the whole weekend if you're nearby and in the mood for some really old hippy reminiscences! And really, who isn't.
key_insight: This is a personal lifestyle post about attending a folk music festival, revealing O'Neil's life outside data science but containing no analytical or thematic content related to her usual topics.
